---
title: "Day 1 - Block 4"
subtitle: "Tools 1"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D1 B4"
    bibliography: ../../../resources/workshop-citations.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

## Tools turn text into action {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Capability grows only with verification.
:::

## Objective

- Explain the tool-calling lifecycle
- Distinguish RAG, skills, MCP
- Complete one verified E3 sprint

## Tool-calling lifecycle

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Task statement] --> B[Scope and constraints]
  B --> C[Choose mechanism]
  C --> D[Plan mode]
  D --> E[Apply minimal patch]
  E --> F[Run checks]
  F --> G[Record trace]
  G --> H[Submit]
  F -->|fails| B
```

## Tool use in decoding

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  P[PROMPT: What time is it?] --> A[The current]
  A --> B1[time (0.6)]
  A --> B2[weather (0.3)]
  B1 -->|call time_tool()| C[is (0.9)]
  C --> D[3:45 PM]
```

## RAG vs Skills vs MCP

| Mechanism | Main use | Failure if misused |
|---|---|---|
| RAG | retrieve missing context | stale/off-topic retrieval |
| Skill | reusable workflow template | cargo-cult execution |
| MCP | standardized tool bridge | bad permissions/mapping |

## IDE with AI vs coding agent

| Feature | IDE with AI | Coding agent |
|---|---|---|
| Autonomy | assists human | can execute plan |
| Tool loop | manual prompting | autonomous calls |
| Verification | mostly human | can self-check + report |

## API vs chat mode

- Chat: fast exploration, low setup
- API: controlled, repeatable workflows
- Pipelines usually need API control

## Tool spotlight: Google Workspace CLI

- Repo: `https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli`
- What it is: terminal tools for Gmail/Drive/Docs/Sheets/Calendar
- How it works: agent calls CLI commands via a shell tool

## Setup in practice

- Install from the repository instructions
- Run auth login once (OAuth)
- Test with one read call before write actions

```bash
# example flow
gws --help
gws auth login
gws calendar agenda
```

## Prompt template for coding sprint

```text
Role: Senior pair programmer.
Task: Fix <bug_id> with minimal blast radius.
Constraints:
1) Show plan before edits.
2) Touch only necessary files.
3) Add/update one failing test first.
4) Return verification trace with residual risk.
Definition of done: checks pass and risk note is explicit.
```

## Hands-on (E3) {background-color="black"}

- Pick one reproducible defect
- Ship minimal patch with one check
- Submit trace: `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=E3`

## Registry references {.smaller}

- Coding evidence: R012
- Agent patterns: R004
- Architecture framing: R003

## Debrief

- What failed first?
- What evidence supports confidence?
- What action stays human-owned?
